questions.question: Explain the difference between implicit and explicit type conversion with examples.
keyPoints: Implicit (widening) - automatic by compiler, smaller to larger type, no data loss
Explicit (narrowing) - requires cast operator, larger to smaller, may lose data
Example implicit: int i = 10; double d = i;
Example explicit: double d = 10.5; int i = (int)d;

questions.question: What is upcasting and downcasting in Java? Explain with a practical example.
keyPoints: Upcasting: Converting subclass reference to superclass reference
Downcasting: Converting superclass reference back to subclass
Upcasting is implicit and safe
Downcasting requires explicit cast and instanceof check
Example: Animal→Dog upcast, Animal→Dog→Dog downcast

questions.question: Explain the concept of autoboxing and unboxing in Java with examples.
keyPoints: Autoboxing: Automatic conversion from primitive to wrapper class
Unboxing: Automatic conversion from wrapper class to primitive
Example autoboxing: Integer i = 10;
Example unboxing: int n = new Integer(10);
Purpose: To use primitives in collections and enable generic programming

questions.question: What is the purpose of the instanceof operator in Java? When should it be used?
keyPoints: Checks if an object is an instance of a class or interface
Returns boolean value
Used before downcasting to prevent ClassCastException
Syntax: object instanceof ClassName
Example: if(obj instanceof String) { String s = (String)obj; }

questions.question: Explain primitive type promotion in Java expressions with examples.
keyPoints: In binary expressions, operands are promoted to a common type
byte, short, char are promoted to int first
Then to larger types if needed
Example: byte + byte = int
Example: char + int = int

questions.question: Write a Java program demonstrating both widening and narrowing conversions.
keyPoints: Program should show widening: int to double
Narrowing with cast: double to int
Show data loss in narrowing
Demonstrate explicit vs implicit conversions
Output showing results

questions.question: What is type casting in the context of inheritance? Explain with code example.
keyPoints: Type casting between related classes in inheritance hierarchy
Upcasting: subclass to superclass (implicit)
Downcasting: superclass to subclass (explicit with cast)
Use instanceof for safety
Code example with Animal and Dog classes

questions.question: Explain Type Conversion and Casting in Java with their types. Write a program demonstrating all types of conversions.
keyPoints: Define type conversion: converting one type to another
Types: Implicit (widening) and Explicit (narrowing)
Explain widening order: byte→short→int→long→float→double
Explain narrowing: requires explicit cast operator
Program showing all conversions with outputs
Explain upcasting and downcasting
Mention autoboxing/unboxing and primitive promotion

questions.question: What is the difference between upcasting and downcasting? Explain with a suitable class hierarchy diagram and code example. Also explain the use of instanceof operator.
keyPoints: Upcasting: subclass to superclass - automatic and safe
Downcasting: superclass to subclass - explicit and requires instanceof check
Class hierarchy example with Animal, Dog, Cat classes
Code demonstrating upcasting and downcasting
instanceof checks before downcasting
ClassCastException handling

questions.question: Explain autoboxing, unboxing, and primitive type promotion in Java with suitable examples and their importance in OOP.
keyPoints: Autoboxing: primitive to wrapper conversion automatically
Unboxing: wrapper to primitive conversion automatically
Examples of both
Primitive promotion in expressions: byte/short/char→int→long→float→double
Importance: Enables use of primitives in collections
Enables generics to work with primitives
Performance implications

questions.question: Write a Java program that demonstrates type casting between related classes in an inheritance hierarchy. Include proper error handling using instanceof.
keyPoints: Create superclass and at least two subclasses
Show upcasting (implicit) from subclass to superclass
Show downcasting (explicit) from superclass to subclass
Use instanceof to check type before downcasting
Handle ClassCastException with try-catch
Demonstrate polymorphism through casting
